top_stories.title: Idira launches as Palo Alto Networks extends CyberArk tech to machine and agentic identities
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks launched Idira, a new identity security platform designed to manage human, machine, and AI agent identities under a single PAM framework. The platform incorporates CyberArk technology to extend privileged access management capabilities to machine and agentic identities.

top_stories.title: Okta extends AI agent security to Amazon Bedrock, opens platform to rival identity providers
top_stories.summary: Okta expanded its Okta for AI Agents platform to support any agent ecosystem, enterprise resource, and identity provider, including a new integration with Amazon Bedrock. The platform extension notably opens support to rival identity providers, broadening Okta's AI agent security coverage beyond its own identity ecosystem.

top_stories.title: Palo Alto Networks Introduces Idira: The Next-Generation Identity Security Platform Built for the AI Enterprise
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks has introduced Idira, a next-generation identity security platform designed for AI-driven enterprise environments. The platform represents Palo Alto Networks' entry into the identity security market, targeting identity and access management challenges specific to AI enterprise deployments.

top_stories.title: Identity breaches are on the rise, and are only getting worse - so how can your business stay safe?
top_stories.summary: CyberArk has issued a warning about the growing threat of identity breaches, highlighting AI agents' access to sensitive files as an emerging risk. The company is advocating for a unified, automated identity security approach to address the increasing frequency and severity of identity-related incidents.

top_stories.title: New Semperis Study Reveals AI's Effects on the Identity Attack Surface
top_stories.summary: Semperis released a study examining how AI agents are expanding the identity attack surface, particularly around Active Directory, Entra ID, and Okta. The research found organizations are granting AI agents access to critical systems before implementing adequate identity security safeguards.

news.title: Inside the REMUS Infostealer: Session Theft, MaaS, and Rapid Evolution
news.summary: The REMUS infostealer malware targets browser sessions and authentication tokens as a Malware-as-a-Service (MaaS) offering, bypassing traditional password-based authentication controls. Flare's analysis details how REMUS has evolved to focus on session theft at scale, directly undermining cookie/token-based authentication and SSO session security.

news.title: CISA Adds Cisco SD-WAN CVE-2026-20182 to KEV After Admin Access Exploits
news.summary: CISA added CVE-2026-20182, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Federal Civilian Executive Branch agencies are required to remediate the issue by May 17, 2026.

news.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller Auth Bypass Actively Exploited to Gain Admin Access
news.summary: Cisco has released patches for a maximum-severity authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-20182, CVSS 10.0) in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and SD-WAN Manager that has been actively exploited in limited attacks. The flaw affects peering authentication mechanisms, allowing attackers to gain administrative access to affected systems.

news.title: Regional routing for AWS access portals: Implementing custom vanity domains for IAM Identity Center
news.summary: AWS has launched multi-Region replication for IAM Identity Center, enabling customers to implement regional routing with custom vanity domains for their access portals. The feature allows workforce users to access a single portal for AWS accounts and applications with improved regional redundancy and custom domain support.

news.title: Interview: AI optimism and upskilling for a shifting job market
news.summary: Okta's chief marketing officer Shannon Duffy shared views on AI's potential as a productivity tool and job market equalizer. The interview focuses on workforce upskilling and AI optimism rather than IAM products, services, or security topics.

news.title: Most Organizations Now Use AI Agents for Sensitive Security Tasks
news.summary: A Semperis study found that most organizations are now deploying AI agents for sensitive security tasks, with 74% believing AI will increase attacks on identity infrastructure. The research highlights growing concern among IAM and security professionals about AI-driven threats targeting identity systems.

news.title: Microsoft Patches 138 Vulnerabilities, Including DNS and Netlogon RCE Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft released patches for 138 security vulnerabilities across its product portfolio, with 30 rated Critical and 104 rated Important. Among the 138 flaws, 61 are privilege escalation vulnerabilities, and the patch set includes critical RCE flaws in DNS and Netlogon, which are relevant to identity infrastructure.

news.title: Keeper Security Delivers Structured Governance for Humans and AI Agents with New PAM Approval Workflows in KeeperPAM
news.summary: Keeper Security has launched new PAM Approval Workflows within its KeeperPAM platform, introducing structured governance and approval-based controls for privileged access. The new Keeper Workflow capability is designed to provide administrators with oversight for both human users and AI agents accessing privileged resources.

news.title: AI threats push Middle East CISOs towards identity-first security
news.summary: Middle East CISOs are shifting toward identity-first security strategies in response to AI-driven threats such as deepfakes and shadow AI. The article covers a regional trend among security leaders moving away from traditional perimeter-based defenses toward identity-centric resilience approaches.

news.title: Analyzing TeamPCP’s Supply Chain Attacks: Checkmarx KICS and elementary-data in CI/CD Credential Theft
news.summary: Researchers analyzed two supply chain attacks by threat actor TeamPCP targeting Checkmarx KICS and elementary-data in April, conducted roughly two days apart. Both incidents involved abuse of CI/CD and release workflows to steal credentials at scale.

news.title: Worm Redux: Fresh Mini Shai-Hulud Infections Bite Supply Chain
news.summary: Hundreds of npm packages in the TanStack open source ecosystem have been infected by a self-propagating, credential-stealing worm attributed to TeamPCP. The worm spreads through the software supply chain and is designed to steal credentials from affected systems.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. The charges relate to SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that enabled the group to compromise at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on stolen authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access.
